compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
# Wire store-wide overlap step into _run_plan_review (LLM-gated, graceful skip, advisory) + ADR

## Why
The overlap check fires in the tolerant window before claim — during plan review — folding results into the output as advisory coaching, WITHOUT ever blocking the claim or altering the claim-gate verdict. This is the integration seam tying enrichment + retrieval + judge into the workflow, and it delivers the epic's ADR.

## What
- Integrate in `_run_plan_review` (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py:401` — the real post-pass site where rising-floor/completion-floor/sidecar/signing run; NOT the wrapper `review_plan` at :352). Overlap findings go in a SEPARATE `overlap[]` verdict key, EXCLUDED from the advisory cap and from the signed `coverage['counts']` — so the ≤3 overlap items never displace real plan-review advisories and the attestation is byte-identical whether overlap is on or off. (This resolves the earlier "inject before counts are baked" tension: we do NOT touch the baked counts at all.)
- Steps: synchronously extract the query ticket's digest by calling S1's `enrich` on the plan-review orchestrator's already-assembled ticket context (the same whole-ticket text `_run_plan_review` passed to its own passes — title+description+comments), so no extra store read is needed, run `retrieve` (S5), run the pairwise `judge` (S6) — the no-embeddings retrieval is justified by the epic's recall experiment (digest-BM25F recall@20 = 1.0), so no embedding index is needed; attach ≤3 results to `overlap[]`. This injection happens AFTER `sidecar.emit()` and the attestation signing, so `overlap[]` is never part of the signed sidecar/coverage (reinforcing verdict-unchanged). Each item is exactly S6's surfaced-finding shape: `{counterpart_id: str, relation: "duplicates"|"supersedes"|"depends_on", shared_artifact: str, confidence: number, link_command: str}`. Whether a candidate is 'overlapping enough' to appear is decided ENTIRELY by S6's aggregation rule (both orderings agree + cited artifact + ≥overlap_conf_threshold); S7 does no thresholding of its own.
- **CLI:** update `_render_plan_review_text` (`src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:386`) to print the `overlap[]` section.
- **MCP:** the `review_plan` MCP tool is `NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT` (returns a plain dict, no `outputSchema`, per `_mcp_llm.py:174` / `schemas/__init__.py:110`) — so there is NO output schema to update; `overlap[]` simply rides in the returned dict. (Corrects the earlier plan text.)
- **Gate/skip:** if `[agents]`, plan-review, or the API key is absent → silent no-op (no `overlap[]`, no error, claim proceeds). Runtime failure in S1/S5/S6 (exception, timeout, empty candidates) → caught, empty `overlap[]`, logged, never blocks.
- **Config flag:** `overlap_enabled` (default OFF) — a BOOL on the `VerifyConfig` dataclass (`src/rebar/_config_schema.py`, `[tool.rebar][verify]`, alongside `require_plan_review_for_claim`) WITH its companion entry in `_SECTIONS["verify"]` (`= _as_bool`) — omitting the `_SECTIONS` entry silently drops the key. (`overlap_enabled` is the ONLY overlap key in `VerifyConfig`; all tunables live on `LLMConfig` per the other stories.)
- **ADR:** write `docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md` capturing Cupid-digest-without-embeddings (+ the recall/latency/cost experiment evidence), event-store-as-enrichment-queue, digest-as-REVIEW_RESULT-sidecar, and overlap-at-review-time-not-as-a-5fd2-criterion. This is the epic's ADR deliverable.

## Dependencies
S1 (enrich), S5 (retrieve), S6 (judge). Sequenced LAST. NOTE: S3 also edits `plan_review/__init__.py` (the certification/enqueue path); this story edits `_run_plan_review` (a different function) — S7 lands after S3 to avoid a merge conflict (depends_on S3).

## Scope
- Edits: `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/__init__.py` (`_run_plan_review` injection), `src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py` (`_render_plan_review_text`), `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` (`VerifyConfig.overlap_enabled` + `_SECTIONS["verify"]` entry), `docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md`.
- **NO schema edit** (resolves the earlier contradiction): `overlap[]` rides in the returned plan-review dict WITHOUT touching `src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json`, which already declares `"additionalProperties": true` (verified) — so the extra `overlap` key validates without a schema change, and the MCP tool is `NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT` anyway. Do NOT add `overlap[]` to the schema file; `test_verdict_schema_accepts_overlap` asserts a verdict dict carrying `overlap[]` still validates against the UNCHANGED `plan_review_verdict` schema.
- OUT: component ops (S1/S5/S6), async enrichment (S2/S3/S4).

## Testing
- `tests/unit/test_plan_review_overlap.py` (FakeRunner + fixture corpus): `test_surfaced` (≤3 in `overlap[]` with link commands); `test_verdict_unchanged` (signed coverage/attestation byte-identical vs overlap-off); `test_query_digest_at_review_time` (digest built from current full text, corpus-independent); `test_graceful_skip` ([agents]/key absent → no `overlap[]`, verdict identical); `test_runtime_error` (judge raises → empty `overlap[]`, no block); `test_flag_off` (`overlap_enabled=off` → skipped); `test_render` (`overlap[]` in CLI text + MCP dict).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `review_plan` returns ≤3 findings in a dedicated `overlap[]` key (each with a `rebar link` command); claim NEVER blocked; signed coverage/attestation byte-identical to overlap-off; `pytest ...::{test_surfaced,test_verdict_unchanged}`.
- [ ] Query digest generated at review time from current full text, independent of the enriched corpus; `::test_query_digest_at_review_time`.
- [ ] `[agents]`/plan-review/key absent → silent no-op, verdict identical; runtime S1/S5/S6 failure → empty `overlap[]`, logged, never blocks; `::{test_graceful_skip,test_runtime_error}`.
- [ ] `overlap_enabled` (bool, default OFF) on `VerifyConfig` WITH its `_SECTIONS["verify"]` coercer gates the step; `::test_flag_off`. `overlap[]` rendered in CLI text and present in the MCP dict (no schema change — tool is NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT); `::test_render`.
- [ ] The ADR `docs/adr/0037-cross-ticket-overlap.md` is written (epic's ADR deliverable), citing the concrete experiment figures (recall@20 = 1.0, single-query 0.69 ms @ 800 digests, ~$1.90 one-time enrichment for 793 tickets) from the epic's experiment comment.
compiled_state.title: Wire store-wide overlap step into review_plan (LLM-gated, graceful skip, advisory surfacing)
